title: Post-UTI follow-up
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.
assessment.rationale: Supports qualified urinary, systemic, pregnancy, sexual, procedure and comorbidity history for post-UTI follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

assessment.text: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.
assessment.rationale: Supports qualified recurrence, fever, flank-pain, vomiting, haematuria and risk-context prompts for post-UTI follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria
source.population: People presenting with dysuria in telehealth, with age, sex, pregnancy, comorbidity, recurrence, and complicated-infection qualifiers.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: UAE-direct for selected dysuria history, red-flag prompts, urinalysis, culture, and qualified renal-testing documentation. Physical examination, diagnosis, medicine, treatment, referral urgency, and non-telehealth pathways remain clinician responsibilities.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

assessment.text: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.
assessment.rationale: Supports qualified urinalysis, culture and renal-function review for post-UTI follow-up; no decision is generated.
